workspaces.title: Workspaces
workspaces.description: Manage Terraform workspaces, state, and runs

form.name: Name
form.namePattern: Letters, numbers, hyphens, and underscores only. Must start with a letter or number.
form.versionTitle: Version in X.Y or X.Y.Z format (e.g. 1.11 or 1.11.5)
form.cpuTitle: Kubernetes CPU quantity: whole cores (1, 2) or millicores (500m, 100m)
form.memoryTitle: Kubernetes memory quantity: bytes (1000) or with suffix (512Mi, 2Gi, 1Ti)
form.vcsRepoUrlTitle: Must be an HTTP or HTTPS URL

stats.filterHealth: Filter to workspaces with health issues

filter.placeholder: Filter by name, label, or status — e.g. "eu1", "env:prod", "status:errored"
filter.label: Label

row.addLabelFilter: Click to filter by this label

empty.none: No workspaces yet. Create one to get started.

auth.subtitle: Sign in to manage your infrastructure
auth.subtitleCli: Sign in to authorize the CLI

errors.missingVerifier: Missing PKCE verifier — please try logging in again

estate.title: Estate topology
estate.description: Every workspace you can see and the modules they use, wired by run-triggers, remote-state, and module links. Group by whatever axis fits your estate — the platform enforces no labeling convention.

errors.load: Failed to load the estate graph.

detail.moduleImpact: {count, plural, one {workspace uses this — bumping it re-plans it} other {workspaces use this — bumping it re-plans them all}}
detail.dependOnThis: {count, plural, one {depends on this} other {depend on this}}

labels.title: Labels
labels.description: Browse labels in use across workspaces, agent pools, registry modules, and registry providers. Read-only — labels are edited on each entity's own page.
labels.emptyKeys: No labels in use yet. Label any workspace, agent pool, registry module, or registry provider to start grouping things here.
labels.emptyValues: No values for "{key}". Either no readable entity carries this label, or it was removed since the last view.

deprecated.body: The Labels browser is no longer in the main navigation and will be removed in a future release. Filter by label from the <workspacesLink>Workspaces</workspacesLink> page instead. If you rely on this page, please <issueLink>open an issue</issueLink>.

tokens.title: API Tokens
tokens.description: Manage authentication tokens for CLI and automation
tokens.tokenReadyHelp: Copy this token now — it will not be shown again.
tokens.selected: {count, plural, one {# token selected} other {# tokens selected}}
tokens.confirmRotate: Rotate this token? The current secret stops working immediately and a new one is issued.

interactive.label: Personal
interactive.help: A personal token for the CLI and ad-hoc automation. Carries your full live permissions.

service_bound.label: Service · bound
service_bound.help: A service token scoped to a subset of your roles. Its access is the intersection of those roles and your own — and it stops working if your account is removed.

service_detached.label: Service · detached
service_detached.help: An admin-managed service token with an absolute pinned scope, bound to no user. Use for critical machine-to-machine automation that must survive any one person leaving.

form.pinnedRolesHelp: — the scope this token is limited to
form.noCustomRoles: No custom roles defined. Create roles under Admin → Roles first.
form.noScopedRoles: You hold no scoped roles to pin. This token would have no access.

sessions.title: Sessions
sessions.descriptionAll: All active sessions across the platform

modules.title: Modules
modules.empty: No modules yet. Create one to get started.

form.name: Name
form.slugTitle: Lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens only. Must start with a lowercase letter.
form.addressNote: Name and provider form the module's registry address and cannot be changed after creation.
form.tagPatternHint: Only tags matching this pattern create versions (e.g. v1.0.0)

providers.title: Providers
providers.empty: No providers yet. Create one to get started.

form.name: Name
form.slugTitle: Lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens only. Must start with a lowercase letter.

moduleDetail.labelLockoutDefault: This label change would reduce your access.
moduleDetail.unlinkConfirm: Unlink this workspace from the module? Impact analysis will no longer run for it.

upload.intro: Select a directory containing your Terraform module files. They will be packaged into a tarball and uploaded.
upload.filesToInclude: {count, plural, one {# file to include:} other {# files to include:}}

vcs.intro: Connect this module to a VCS repository. New versions are published automatically when semver tags matching the tag pattern are pushed. Branch commits alone do not create versions.
vcs.tagPatternHint: Only tags matching this pattern create versions (e.g. v1.0.0)

vcsStatus.firstVersionHint: Push a semver tag (e.g. v1.0.0) matching the tag pattern to publish the first version.

links.title: Linked Workspaces
links.description: Linked workspaces receive speculative plans when PRs are opened against this module, and standard runs when new versions are published.

interface.title: Inputs & Outputs
interface.noData: No interface data available for this version.
interface.name: Name
interface.noneDeclared: This module version declares no inputs or outputs.

versions.title: Versions
versions.emptyVcs: No versions yet. Push a semver tag matching "{pattern}" to the connected repository to publish a version.
versions.emptyUpload: No versions yet. Upload a module or connect VCS to get started.

deleteDialog.title: Confirm Delete
deleteDialog.moduleBody: This will permanently delete this module and all its versions.

providerDetail.publishNote: Provider versions are published with the terrapod-publish CLI, which client-signs the binaries. Uploading and signing provider binaries from the browser is no longer supported. This page is read-only for versions and platforms.
providerDetail.empty: No versions yet. Publish with the terrapod-publish CLI to get started.
providerDetail.labelLockoutDefault: This label change would reduce your access.

deleteDialog.title: Confirm Delete
deleteDialog.providerBody: This will permanently delete this provider and all its versions.
deleteDialog.versionBody: This will permanently delete version {version} and its platforms.
deleteDialog.platformBody: This will permanently delete the {os}/{arch} platform.

confirm.delete: Delete user "{email}"? This cannot be undone.

adminRoles.description: Manage RBAC roles and user role assignments

form.name: Name
form.namePattern: Letters, numbers, hyphens, and underscores only. Must start with a letter or number.

caps.selected: {count, plural, one {# capability} other {# capabilities}} selected

presetHelp.before: Selecting a preset above pre-checks its capabilities. Ticking or unticking a box past a preset marks the role
presetHelp.after: and its grant is sent as an explicit capability set.

confirm.deleteRole: Delete role "{name}"? Any assignments referencing it are affected.

adminVcs.title: VCS Connections
adminVcs.confirmDelete: Delete this VCS connection? Workspaces using it will lose VCS integration. This cannot be undone.

form.name: Name
form.namePattern: Letters, numbers, hyphens, and underscores only. Must start with a letter or number.
form.providerImmutable: Provider is immutable — delete and recreate to change it
form.serverUrlHint: Must be an HTTP or HTTPS URL (e.g. https://github.mycompany.com)
form.appIdHint: GitHub App ID — numeric digits only

-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----
...

Drop a .pem file here or click Browse
form.webhookSecretSet: Set — leave blank to keep, enter a new value to rotate
form.webhookSecretGlobal: Leave blank to use the global webhook secret
form.webhookSecretHelp: Per-connection HMAC secret for this installation's webhooks. When unset, the global secret is used.
form.accessTokenKeep: Leave blank to keep the current token

adminVariableSets.title: Variable Sets
adminVariableSets.description: Manage org-scoped variable sets for workspace groups

form.name: Name
form.namePattern: Letters, numbers, hyphens, and underscores only. Must start with a letter or number.

detail.deleteDescription: Permanently delete this variable set and all its variables.
detail.globalBanner: This variable set is global and applies to all workspaces automatically.
detail.emptyWorkspaces: No workspaces assigned to this variable set.
detail.confirmDeleteVar: Delete this variable? This cannot be undone.
detail.confirmRemoveWorkspace: Remove this workspace from the variable set? Its variables will no longer apply to that workspace.

adminAutodiscovery.title: Autodiscovery
adminAutodiscovery.description: Auto-create workspaces in monorepos when PRs touch matching paths
adminAutodiscovery.empty: No autodiscovery rules yet. Create one to start auto-provisioning workspaces from PRs in your monorepo.

form.name: Name
form.matchPatternHint: Gitignore-style globs. ** matches across path segments. Only *.tf* files trigger autodiscovery.
form.nameTemplateHint: Use for the directory with / replaced by -, or for the directory as-is. Empty = default (just the dashed path).
form.executionModeHint: Autodiscovery is VCS-driven; only agent execution is supported.
form.onDeleteFlag: Flag (safe — mark pending deletion, requires manual action)
form.onDeleteDestroy: Destroy (DANGER — tears down infrastructure then archives the workspace)
form.onDirectoryDeleteHint: What happens to an autodiscovered workspace when its directory is removed on the tracked branch.
form.onDeleteDestroyWarning: This will run a real terraform destroy on the workspace when its directory is removed on the tracked branch.
form.executionHooks: Execution hooks (associated with each new workspace)
form.executionHooksHint: Pick from hooks defined on the <page>Execution Hooks</page> admin page — each selected hook is associated with every workspace this rule creates.
form.noHooks: No execution hooks defined yet. Create one on the Execution Hooks admin page first.
form.runTaskTemplates: Run task templates (created on each new workspace)
form.notificationTemplates: Notification templates (created on each new workspace)
form.previewTitle: Walk the repo and preview which workspaces this rule WOULD create — no persistence

table.name: Name
table.previewTitle: Walk the repo and preview which workspaces this rule would create

deleteModal.title: Delete rule?
deleteModal.body: Workspaces auto-created by this rule keep working. Future PRs touching new paths under this rule won't auto-create more workspaces.

preview.title: Preview: {ruleName}
preview.noMatches: No directories in this repo match the rule's pattern. Nothing to create.
preview.skipCollisionTitle: A workspace with this name already exists for another reason
preview.willCreate: {count, plural, one {# workspace will be created.} other {# workspaces will be created.}} Existing workspaces (collisions) are left untouched.
preview.saveToProvision: Save the rule to provision these workspaces.

success.provisioned: {count, plural, one {Created # workspace from "{ruleName}"} other {Created # workspaces from "{ruleName}"}}

errors.previewPrereq: Set VCS connection, repo URL, and pattern before previewing

adminBulkUpdate.title: Bulk Update
adminBulkUpdate.description: Select workspaces by filter, then apply a settings change across all of them

update.heading: 2. Apply changes
update.hint: Only fields you set below are changed. The filter above selects which workspaces receive the change.
update.setLabels: Set labels (replaces the labels map on every matched workspace)
update.setRunTasks: Set run tasks (upserted by name on every matched workspace)
update.dryRun: Dry run (preview only — no changes written)
update.confirmWarning: This writes changes to every matched workspace.

dryResult.heading: Dry run result
dryResult.summary: Matched {matched}; {wouldChange} would change; {unchanged} unchanged. Nothing was written.
dryResult.noChange: No workspaces would change with this update.

applyResult.heading: Apply result
applyResult.summary: Matched {matched}; {applied} applied; {unchanged} unchanged; {errors, plural, one {# error} other {# errors}}.

adminExecutionHooks.title: Execution Hooks
adminExecutionHooks.description: Custom shell steps run inside runner Jobs at pre/post-plan/apply points, associated with workspaces

form.name: Name
form.namePatternTitle: Letters, numbers, hyphens, and underscores only. Must start with a letter or number.
form.descriptionPlaceholder: Add an internal hosts entry before init
form.priorityTitle: Lower runs first when several hooks share a point (ties broken by name).
form.scriptHelp: Runs with the runner's cloud identity. A non-zero exit fails the run. Secrets should come from workspace variables, not inline here.

adminPolicySets.description: OPA policy-as-code enforcement, scoped to workspaces by label

form.name: Name
form.namePatternTitle: Letters, numbers, hyphens, and underscores only. Must start with a letter or number.
form.helpVcs: Policies will be synced from the repository automatically.
form.helpInline: Add policies and refine label scoping after creating the set.

form.name: Name
form.nameTitle: Letters, numbers, hyphens, and underscores only. Must start with a letter or number.
form.anyPool: Any agent pool may be used.
form.variableOptionsHint: Per-input overrides (options, hidden, default). Leave as [] if unused.

confirm.delete: Delete this catalog item? Existing provisioned instances are unaffected.

form.name: Name
form.parametersHint: Each parameter becomes a provision form field. Leave as [] if unused.

adminAgentPools.title: Agent Pools
adminAgentPools.description: Manage runner agent pools and their listeners

form.name: Name
form.namePattern: Letters, numbers, hyphens, and underscores only. Must start with a letter or number.

badges.degradedTooltip: Listeners are heartbeating but their certificates have expired — runs will fail to launch.

danger.title: Delete Pool
danger.description: Permanently delete this agent pool and all associated tokens.

created.title: Token created successfully
created.copyNow: Copy this token now — it will not be shown again.

form.maxUsesHint: Default 2 — tolerates one bootstrap-race retry across listener replicas.

listeners.empty: No listeners registered for this pool.

tooltips.certExpired: Listener is heartbeating but its certificate has expired — every cert-authenticated call (claim run, renew, runner-token) will fail. Most likely cause: the API never registered the latest cert, or the cert TTL elapsed before renewal.
tooltips.degraded: Listeners are heartbeating but their certificates have expired — runs will fail to launch.

confirm.revokeToken: Revoke this join token? Listeners using it can no longer join, and this cannot be undone.
confirm.deleteListener: Delete this listener? It will re-register on its next heartbeat if the pod is still running.

adminAuditLog.title: Audit Log
adminAuditLog.description: Immutable log of all API requests

pagination.summary: Page {current} of {total} ({count, plural, one {# entry} other {# entries}})

adminBinaryCache.title: Cache
adminBinaryCache.description: CLI binary and provider cache management

warm.heading: Warm cache
warm.help: Pre-pull binaries and provider platforms into the cache (one per line). Trailing os/arch tokens are optional — omit them to use the configured default platforms.
warm.confirm: Warm the selected cache entries now?
warm.errorEmpty: Add at least one binary or provider line to warm.

purge.confirmBinary: Purge {tool} {version} from the cache? It will be re-fetched from upstream on the next request.
purge.confirmProvider: Purge {provider} {version} from the provider cache? It will be re-fetched from upstream on the next request.

batchPurge.confirmBinary: Purge all selected binary cache entries? They will be re-fetched from upstream on the next request.
batchPurge.confirmProvider: Purge all selected provider cache entries? They will be re-fetched from upstream on the next request.

catalog.title: Service Catalog
catalog.description: Provision self-service infrastructure from curated modules

catalogDetail.notFound: Catalog item not found, or the service catalog is not enabled.

pending.banner: The run for {name} is planned and waiting for review. Confirm to apply, or discard.

provision.title: Provision
provision.workspaceNameTitle: Letters, numbers, hyphens, and underscores only. Must start with a letter or number.
provision.noPoolsAvailable: No agent pools are available to you. You need write access to an agent pool to provision — ask an administrator to grant it.
provision.noPoolsAllowed: None of the agent pools you can access are allowed by this catalog item. Ask an administrator to widen the item’s allowed pools or grant you access to one of them.
provision.autoApplyHint: Auto-apply applies changes without a manual review step.

instances.title: Instances
instances.empty: No instances provisioned from this item yet.
instances.orphanTitle: Delete the catalog record without destroying its infrastructure (discouraged)

reconfigure.title: Reconfigure {name}
reconfigure.description: Update inputs and/or version, then queue a run.

destroy.title: Destroy {name}
destroy.heading: Destroy {name}?
destroy.description: This queues a destroy run that tears down this instance's infrastructure. On a successful apply the workspace is archived. This cannot be undone.
destroy.autoApplyHint: Auto-apply tears down immediately without review.

orphan.title: Orphan {name}
orphan.heading: Orphan {name}?
orphan.description: This deletes the catalog instance record but does <warn>not</warn> destroy its infrastructure — the provisioned resources keep running, <warn>untracked and unmanaged</warn>. This is discouraged. To reclaim the infrastructure, cancel and use <emph>Destroy</emph> instead.
orphan.confirmPrompt: Type the instance name <mono>{name}</mono> to confirm:

actions.provisioned: Provisioned {name} — plan is ready for review.
actions.orphaned: Orphaned {name} — catalog record deleted; its infrastructure is left running, untracked and unmanaged.

sessionExpiry.banner: Session expires in {minutes, plural, one {# minute} other {# minutes}} — <link>re-authenticate</link>

tokenExpiry.withinDays: {count, plural, one {# service token} other {# service tokens}} expiring within {days, plural, one {# day} other {# days}} — <link>review</link>
tokenExpiry.soon: {count, plural, one {# service token} other {# service tokens}} expiring soon — <link>review</link>

regenerate.label: Regenerate
regenerate.tooltip: Re-run the AI summary against the same plan inputs
regenerate.confirm: Regenerate the AI analysis? This runs the model again.

badges.toAdd: {count, plural, one {# to add} other {# to add}}

badges.toImport: {count, plural, one {# to import} other {# to import}}

chat.sharedThreadNote: One shared thread, visible to anyone with workspace read.
chat.placeholder: Ask a follow-up question about this plan… (⌘/Ctrl + Enter to send)

planSummary.translatedNote: Automatically translated to your language. The authoritative summary is the original.

relTime.minutesAgo: {count, plural, one {# minute ago} other {# minutes ago}}

relTime.daysAgo: {count, plural, one {# day ago} other {# days ago}}

activity.plannedConfirmable: Waiting for you to confirm & apply

confirm.apply: Apply this run? This applies the plan and changes infrastructure.

banner.destroy: This is a destroy run — all managed resources will be destroyed when applied.
banner.planOnlyCli: This is a plan-only run initiated from the CLI on a VCS-connected workspace. Apply is not available for CLI-uploaded code — only VCS-managed code can be applied.
banner.moduleOverridesSuffix: will be fetched from the PR branch instead of the published registry version.

log.colorOnTitle: Showing ANSI colors — click for plain text
log.colorOffTitle: Showing plain text — click for colors
log.followOnTitle: Following the live tail — click to stop
log.endTitle: Jump to the end of the log

policyPanel.heading: Policy Checks
policyPanel.blockedMessage: This run is blocked by a mandatory policy set. It will not apply until the failure is resolved or an admin overrides it.
policyPanel.noDenyMessage: Policy failed without producing a deny message or error output

lifecycle.pendingDeletionDefault: This workspace is marked for deletion and requires manual action.

settings.title: Settings
settings.renamedNotice: Workspace renamed. Update the <name></name> field in your <cloud></cloud> block to match.

fields.name: Name
fields.nameTitle: Letters, numbers, hyphens, underscores. Must start with a letter or number.
fields.cpuRequestTitle: Kubernetes CPU quantity: whole cores (1, 2) or millicores (500m, 100m)
fields.memoryRequestTitle: Kubernetes memory quantity: bytes (1000) or with suffix (512Mi, 2Gi, 1Ti)
fields.versionTitle: Version in X.Y or X.Y.Z format (e.g. 1.11 or 1.11.5)
fields.terragruntVersionTitle: Terragrunt version in X.Y or X.Y.Z format (e.g. 1.0)
fields.vcsRepositoryTitle: Must be an HTTP or HTTPS URL
fields.labelsTitle: Workspace labels do double duty: label-based RBAC matching, and as the "tags" matched by terraform/tofu cloud '{' workspaces '{' tags = ... '}' '}' blocks.
fields.triggerPrefixesHint: Directories that trigger runs. Overrides working directory filtering when set.
fields.driftIgnoreRulesHint: Glob-aware Terraform address + attribute paths suppressed by the drift classifier. <star></star> matches zero or more non-<dot></dot> chars (spans <n></n> indices); <starIdx></starIdx> matches any bracketed index. A bare address with no attribute suffix silences any change to that resource — including destroys. Affects drift only, not regular plan/apply. See <docs>drift-ignore-rules.md</docs> for the full grammar.
fields.driftIgnoreRulesNone: None (every plan diff counts as drift)

vcsWorkflowWarning.title: Authorization delegated to your VCS repository.
vcsWorkflowWarning.rbac: Anyone who can merge the PR can apply. Branch protection rules (required reviews, status checks, code owner approval) become the gate. Terrapod role/label RBAC doesnot apply to comment-driven actions in this mode.
vcsWorkflowWarning.recommended: Recommended: require linear history (rebase/squash before merge) so the commit you apply is the commit that lands.
vcsWorkflowWarning.credit: Credit: this workflow is modelled on <atlantis>Atlantis</atlantis>. Atlantis remains the right tool for teams who only want PR-comment-driven applies and no platform UI.

lock.title: Lock Status
lock.lockedDesc: This workspace is locked. No plans or applies can run.
lock.unlockedDesc: This workspace is unlocked and ready for runs.

drift.title: Drift Detection
drift.dismissTitle: Clear the reported drift state. The next scheduled check will repopulate it from reality.
drift.checkNowTitle: Queue a plan-only run to check for drift

planExpiry.title: Plan Expiry
planExpiry.description: Auto-discard an unconfirmed plan once it is older than this, so a stale plan can't be applied against a moved world. Off by default; the plan must then be re-run. (Applies to apply-capable runs only.)

aiSummary.title: AI Plan Summary
aiSummary.description: Automatic LLM-generated change summary, risk assessment, and failure analysis on every plan-phase outcome for this workspace.
aiSummary.contextPlaceholder: Facts the AI should know about this workspace specifically. e.g. 'Fronts the vault for service X — destroying the KMS key causes a global outage.'
aiSummary.contextHint: Added on top of deployment-wide context. Max 4000 characters.

slack.title: Slack notifications
slack.description: Opt this workspace into Slack run notifications — approval requests, applies, errors, and drift. Leave the channel empty and this workspace stays silent (there is no deployment-wide fan-out). Requires the Slack app to be enabled by an administrator.
slack.hint: The Slack app must already be a member of the channel.

delete.title: Delete Workspace
delete.description: Permanently delete this workspace and all associated state, variables, and runs.

variables.empty: No variables configured for this workspace.

runs.queuePlanConfirm: Queue a speculative plan for this workspace?

state.deleteConfirm: Delete state version #{serial}? This cannot be undone.

confirm.removeWorkspace: Remove this workspace from the hook? The hook will no longer run for it.

confirm.prompt: Link this Slack account to your Terrapod identity? Every future Slack action (approve/discard) will run as this Terrapod user with their permissions.
confirm.warning: Only continue if you just started this link from Slack yourself. If you didn't, close this tab and don't link — this binds the Slack account above to your Terrapod identity.

success.message: Your Slack account is now linked to Terrapod.
